Package org.eventb.core

Interface IIdentifierElement

All Superinterfaces:
org.eclipse.core.runtime.IAdaptable, IElementManipulation, IInternalElement, IParent, IRodinElement, ISnapshotable
All Known Subinterfaces:
ICarrierSet, IConstant, IParameter, IVariable
All Known Implementing Classes:
CarrierSet, Constant, Parameter, Variable
public interface IIdentifierElement extends IInternalElement
Common protocol for elements that contain an Event-B identifier (carrier set, constant or variable).

The identifier is manipulated as a bare string of characters, as there is no guarantee that it is parseable (i.e., a syntactically correct identifier).

The attribute storing the identifier string is optional. This means if the attribute is not present, the value should be interpreted as undefined.

This interface is not intended to be implemented by clients.

Since:
1.0

  • Method Details

    • hasIdentifierString

      boolean hasIdentifierString() throws RodinDBException
      Tests whether the identifier string is defined or not.
      Returns:
      whether the identifier string is defined or not
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if there was a problem accessing the database

    • getIdentifierString

      String getIdentifierString() throws RodinDBException
      Returns the string representation of the identifier contained in this element.
      Returns:
      the identifier of this element as a string
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if there was a problem accessing the database

    • setIdentifierString

      void setIdentifierString(String identifier, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Sets the string representation of the identifier contained in this element.
      Parameters:
      identifier - the string representation of the identifier
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if there was a problem accessing the database
